content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 7,
1993
Tony Miller
Chief Deputy
Office of the Secretary of State
1230 J Street
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-93-266
Dear Mr. Miller:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Office of the Secretary of State regarding the mass
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) .
You
have specifically requested informal assistance.
2
Please note that your letter and our response are public
records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a
proper request for disclosure.
QUESTION
May the election ballot pamphlet be distributed with public
funds where the ballot will contain a personal letter signed by
you,
and a reference to the Attorney General,, Daniel E.
Lungren?
CONCLUS ION
Section 89001 does not restrict the use of nonelected
officials in mass mailings.
Moreover,
a mass mailing that
includes the name of an elected official who is not affiliated
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—93—266
Page
2
with the agency sending the mailing, is not subject to the
restrictions of Section 89001 and Regulation 18901.
DISCUSSION
On March 6,
1990,
you received specific advice regarding the
content of the ballot pamphlet distributed by the Office of the
Secretary of State
(Miller Advice Letter,
No.
1—90—138.).
You
have asked whether, consistent with the advice provided to you in
1990,
you may add several references to specific public officials
to the pamphlet,
including their names and signatures.
One of the
names you wish to add is that of the elected Attorney General,
Daniel E.
Lungren.
As we discussed in our telephone conversation of July 2,
1993,
Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass
mailing shall be sent at public expense.
However, Regulation
18901(a) clarifies that Section 89001 only prohibits a mailing j
all of the following apply:
(1)
Any item sent is delivered,
by any means,
to the recipient at his or her residence, place of
employment or business,
or post office box.
For
purposes of this subdivision
(a) (1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record,
or button,
or a
written document.
(2)
The item sent either:
(A)
Features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing,
or
(B)
Includes the name, office,
photograph,
or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing,
and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination,
or concert with the elected
officer;
(3) (A)
Any of the costs of distribution is
paid for with public moneys;
or
(B)
Costs of design, production,
and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
moneys,
and the design, production,
or
printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this
regulation.
(4)
More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent,
in a single calendar month,


Our File No.
1-93-266
Page
3
excluding any item sent in response to an
V
unsolicited request and any item described in
subdivision (b);
Regulation 18901(a).
Thus, Regulation 18901 does not affect a public agency’s
inclusion of the names of appointed,
and other nonelected public
officials.
With respect to the reference to Mr.
Lungren, Regulation
18901(a) (2) further narrows the application, of the prohibition to
elected officers who are affiliated with the agency which produces
or sends the mailing.
An elected officer is “affiliated” with an
agency if the elected officer:
(1) is a member, officer,
or
employee of the agency,
or of a subunit thereof such as a
committee; or
(2) has supervisory control over the agency; or
(3)
appoints one or more members of the agency.
(Regulation
18901(c) (1).)
Since Attorney General Lungren is not affiliated with the
Office of the Secretary of State, the use of the Attorney
V
General’s name in the ballot pamphlet is not restricted by Section
89001.
V
Please note, however, that this advice is limited to the
specific questions raised in your request and the facts provided.
3
Moreover, this advice is limited to the provisions of the
Political Reform Act.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
V
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
V
1 Counsel
By:\ John W. Wallace
Counsel,
Legal Division
The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing
•advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1 •FPPC Ops.
71.)
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10c per page.
